AVR16EB20._notes: Aux Flash: JSON=16 and DB=8, both WRONG (no aux flash in this family). JSON 16 is likely confusing main flash size.

PIC32CM1602GV00032._notes: DB had wrong variant data â€” Flash=32 (should be 16), IO=52 (should be 26, that's 64-pin), I2C=6/SPI=5 (should be 4/4, those are 48/64-pin values). DB likely copied 64-pin variant values into 32-pin part.

PIC32CM3204GV00032._notes: DB has same variant-mixing issue as 1602: IO=52 (64-pin value), I2C=6 (48/64-pin), DACs=4 (wrong). JSON had SPI=6 and ADC=20 (64-pin values for 32-pin part).

AVR16EB28._notes: MCP first miss: IO Pins 27 vs actual 24. JSON wrong on Aux Flash=16, ADC=24 (32-pin value), Input Capture=16, CCL=1, Periph Trig=No, RTC=No. DB wrong on SPI=2, IrDA=Yes, Timers=4, QEI=1, PWM=4, PWM bases=2, CCL=1, POR=No, Vbat=Yes.

PIC32CM1602GV00048._notes: CRITICAL: DB has WRONG CPU â€” says Cortex-M23 but GV00 family is Cortex-M0+. LE00 family is M23, someone mixed up. DB also has IO=52 (64-pin value), SPI=5, wrong timers, POR=No, Vbat=Yes.

PIC32CM3204GV00048._notes: MCP second miss: TempMax=85 (only I-grade), should include E-grade=125. DB: IO=52 (64-pin), SPI=5, DACs=4, wrong timers, POR=No, Vbat=Yes. JSON: ADC=20 (64-pin), DACs=2, DAC Res=12.

AVR32DU20._notes: MCP data was empty (progress overwritten). Both JSON and DB got CCL LUTs wrong (1 vs 4). DB had DACs=05 (typo?), PTC=PTC (not present), CPU=AVR32 (should be AVR).

PIC16F17526._notes: MCP misses: Timers=3 (should be 2), PWM=10 (should be 4). JSON wrong: SPI=1, ADC=35, Pincount=16, IO=14. DB wrong: USART=1, ADC=35, QEI=1, PWM Res=10, POR=No.

AVR16DU14._notes: MCP: PWM Time Bases=3 (should be 1), LIN/IrDA reported as count '2' instead of 'Yes'. JSON: CCL LUTs=1 (should be 4). DB: DACs=1 (should be 0), Motor Control=3 (should be 0), CCL LUTs=1, BOR=Yes (should be Programmable BOR).

_winner_tally.MCP: 17/27 (WORST so far â€” pulled wrong variant data)

PIC16F17554._notes: CRITICAL: MCP pulled PIC16F17556 (28K) data for PIC16F17554 (7K). Flash=28 should be 7, RAM=2 should be 0.5, OpAmp=1 should be 3. DB also had wrong flash/RAM (same mistake), plus Aux Flash=256 (absurd). JSON got Flash=7 correct but ADC=35 (all-pin value).

PIC32CM1602GV00064._notes: DB has Flash=64 for 1602 part (should be 16 â€” '1602' means 16K/2K). This is the 64-pin variant so DB got peripheral counts right for once. MCP recurring TempMax=85 (I-grade only). JSON TempMax=105 (weird value).

PIC32CM3204GV00064._notes: 32-bit timers: all 3 sources wrong â€” MCP=0, DB=2, should be 4 (8 TCs pair into 4x32-bit). DB got peripheral counts right since this IS the 64-pin variant. DB still has Aux Flash=8, POR=No, BOR=Yes pattern.

AVR16DU20._notes: Same patterns as other DU parts: MCP PWM=8/bases=3 wrong, IO=14 (should be 15). DB: DACs=05, Motor=3, Vbat=Yes, BOR=Yes.

PIC16F17546._notes: DB worst: IO=36 and Pincount=28 (wrong variant â€” 44-pin values?), USART=1, LIN=No, ADC=35, ADC Res=18(!), PWM=2, PWM Res=10, POR=No. MCP: Timers=3 (should be 2), Motor=1 (should be 2).

PIC16F17555._notes: DB: Flash=28 (should be 14), RAM=2 (should be 1), Aux=256(!), IO=36, ADC=35, PWM=2, POR=No. MCP: BOR=Yes instead of Programmable BOR.

AVR32DU14._notes: DB has WRONG variant: Flash=16 (should be 32), RAM=2 (should be 4), Pincount=32 (should be 14!), Packages=TQFP/VQFN (should be SOIC). DB mixed up AVR32DU14 with AVR32DU32.

PIC16F18174.source: NEEDS SEPARATE DATASHEET â€” not yet verified
PIC16F18174._notes: Different family from PIC16F175xx. JSON says Flash=7, RAM=0.5, Pincount=40. DB says Flash=28, RAM=0.5. MCP says Flash=28, RAM=2. Need datasheet to resolve.

PIC16F17174.source: NEEDS SEPARATE DATASHEET â€” not yet verified
PIC16F17174._notes: Different family. JSON says Flash=7, RAM=0.5. MCP says Flash=7, RAM=0.5. DB says Flash=28, RAM=2. MCP+JSON likely correct for flash/RAM. DB has wrong variant data again.

AVR32DU28._notes: MCP overcounted ADC channels (21 is 32-pin value, 28-pin has 17). Same PWM overcounting pattern. JSON best run â€” only CCL LUTs wrong.

AVR16DU32._notes: JSON confused '32' in part name â€” AVR16DU32 means 16KB flash in 32-pin package. CCL LUTs=4 all three agreed for first time on DU.

_winner_tally.MCP: 21/25 (I2C=2 wrong, IO=25 wrong variant, Periph Trig=6 not Yes)
_winner_tally.DB: 21/28 (RAM=8, I2C=2, LIN=No, Timers=6, Motor=3, CCL=0, Touch=PTC, WWDT=No, POR=No)

AVR32SD28._notes: NEW FAMILY: AVR SD (functional safety, dual-core lockstep). I2C: both MCP+DB said 2, datasheet Table 2 says 1 for 28-pin (2 is 32-pin only). DB: RAM=8 is 64KB variant, Touch=PTC wrong (SD has no PTC), CCL=0 should be 6.

_winner_tally.MCP: 18/23 (I2C=2, USART=3, LIN=3, Packages wrong â€” all wrong-variant pulls)
_winner_tally.DB: 23/30 (I2C=2, LIN=No, Motor=3, CCL=0, Touch=PTC, POR=No, Vbat=Yes)

AVR32SD20._notes: MCP pulled 28/32-pin peripheral counts for 20-pin part: USART=3 (should be 2), I2C=2 (should be 1). DB: CCL=0 should be 6, Touch=PTC wrong (SD has no PTC). Both MCP+DB got I2C wrong.

_winner_tally.JSON: 23/28 (SPI=1, ADC=35, IO=26, PWM bases=1, Input Cap=0)
_winner_tally.DB: 22/31 (Aux=4, IO=35, LIN=No, ADC=35, QEI=1, PWM Res=10, Periph Trig=Yes, POR=No, BOR=Yes)

PIC16F17556._notes: Same family as PIC16F17526. ADC channels: datasheet says 24 (28-pin ext+), MCP said 22 (close), JSON+DB both said 35 (all-pin family max). DB consistent pattern: Aux Flash wrong, POR=No, BOR=Yes, LIN=No.

_winner_tally.MCP: 22/28 (IO=35, Pincount=40/44, DAC Out=1, Timers=3, PWM=6, PWM bases=3)

PIC16F18175._notes: PIC16F181xx has NO Op Amp (171xx does). JSON feed has I2C=0 and SPI=0 for this family â€” data gap. MCP overcounted PWM again (6 vs 4) and timers (3 vs 2).

_winner_tally.DB: 23/31 (Aux=8, RAM=2, IO=28, LIN=No, OpAmp=3, QEI=1, Periph Trig=Yes, BOR=Yes)

PIC16F17175._notes: DB had OpAmp=3 (should be 1 â€” 171xx has 1, 175xx has 3). DB IO=28 wrong for 40-pin (should be 36). JSON missing I2C/SPI again for this family.

AVR32DU32._notes: DB: CPU=AVR32 (should be AVR), DACs=05, CCP=04, Motor=3, BOR=Yes, Vbat=Yes. JSON only missed CCL LUTs=1.

PIC16F18176._notes: Same 181xx family (no OpAmp). MCP: IO=35, Pincount=40/44, Timers=3. JSON: I2C=0, SPI=0 gap. DB: Aux=4, LIN=No, QEI=1, Periph Trig=Yes, BOR=Yes.

PIC16F17176._notes: Same 171xx family (1 OpAmp). DB: OpAmp=3 (should be 1), IO=28 (should be 36). MCP: PWM=8, DAC Out=1, overcounting again.

PIC16F17574._notes: DB: Flash=28 and RAM=2 (should be 7 and 0.5 — pulled 28K variant data for 7K part). MCP overcounted PWM=10, Timers=3, Motor=1.

PIC16F17575._notes: DB: Flash=28 (should be 14), RAM=2 (should be 1), Aux=2 — same wrong-variant pattern. MCP: IO=35 (should be 36), Pincount=40 (should be 44).

PIC16F17576._notes: DB best run (87%) because this IS the max memory variant — no variant-mixing errors. Both JSON+DB got SPI=1 (should be 2). POR=Yes in DB for first time! MCP still overcounts timers and DAC outputs.

PIC16F18114._notes: Smallest pin count PIC16 so far (8-pin). DB: IO=18 (20-pin value for 8-pin part!), Motor=4 (should be 1). JSON: ADC=11, PWM/CCP undercounted. MCP strong at 93%.

PIC16F18124._notes: DB WORST EVER (51.6%) — pulled 20-pin 14K variant data for 14-pin 7K part. 15 fields wrong including Flash, RAM, Pincount, IO, I2C, USART, OpAmp. BOR all 3 agreed (Programmable BOR) for first time.

PIC16F17124._notes: DB: Flash=28 (should be 7), IO=28 (should be 12), OpAmp=3 (should be 1), CCL=2 (should be 4), SPI=1 — massive variant mixing. JSON: I2C=0, SPI=0 gap, ADC=17 (20-pin value). MCP: BOR=Yes (should be Prog BOR), PWM=6 overcounted.

PIC16F18125._notes: MCP empty — no extraction for this part. JSON+DB both ~65%, tied. Both got ADC=17 wrong (should be 11 for 14-pin). Without MCP as tiebreaker, many fields unresolvable. Shows value of MCP data.

PIC16F18154._notes: JSON+DB both had Flash=28 (should be 7) — MCP got it right. DB had 13 wrong fields (58%). JSON I2C=0, SPI=0 gap continues. MCP overcounted timers=3, PWM=6 as usual.
